articles.title: House breaks with Trump on surveillance, forcing short-term extension amid GOP revolt
articles.summary: A late-night revolt in the House sent a divided surveillance fight into a 15-day reprieve, exposing Republican fractures and setting up another deadline showdown by month’s end.

articles.title: March inflation jolts markets as gasoline shock collides with tariff debate
articles.summary: A hotter-than-expected March CPI report, a jump in gas prices and rising consumer inflation expectations are complicating the Federal Reserve’s path just as policymakers weigh how much tariff pressure is feeding into prices.

articles.title: NASA says April’s bright comet may be the month’s most-watched sky event
articles.summary: C/2025 R3 is due for its closest approach later this month, giving observers with binoculars or a small telescope a chance to catch a newly active comet amid April’s meteor-shower season.

articles.title: CinemaCon 2026’s biggest surprise: Amazon MGM turns to nostalgia, star power and a bigger theatrical swing
articles.summary: The studio used its Las Vegas showcase to unveil new looks at Spaceballs 2, The Thomas Crown Affair and more, signaling how aggressively it wants to grow its movie slate after Project Hail Mary’s breakout.

articles.title: OpenAI pushes child-safety blueprint as AI companies race to answer abuse fears
articles.summary: OpenAI’s new child-protection proposal underscores a bigger shift in AI: the industry is trying to prove that safety work can move as fast as model capabilities.

articles.title: White House pushes light-touch AI rules as states race ahead, setting up a tech policy fight in 2026
articles.summary: The administration’s new framework asks Congress to avoid a patchwork of state AI laws, but lawmakers, regulators and industry are already moving on their own.

articles.title: Why Europe’s new age-verification push is becoming a culture war over childhood online
articles.summary: As the EU rolls out an age-verification app and more countries tighten rules for minors, a once-technical policy debate is turning into a fast-moving fight over privacy, platform design and what it means to grow up online.

articles.title: Lebanon truce takes hold as US-Iran talks enter precarious final stretch
articles.summary: A short ceasefire in Lebanon is easing one front in a wider regional crisis, but the real test is whether Washington and Tehran can still bridge nuclear and security gaps before the truce expires.

articles.title: Coachella 2026 turns into a weekend of pop-culture collision, from headline sets to viral flashpoints
articles.summary: As the first weekend of Coachella’s 2026 edition winds down, the festival is already shaping the spring’s pop-culture conversation with star power, surprise moments and a fresh round of online debate.

articles.title: US-brokered Lebanon truce offers the first real pause in a widening regional war
articles.summary: A 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah is bringing displaced families home, but the deal’s narrow terms and excluded players show how fragile any wider peace remains.
